name: United Finance
description_short: Family-owned consumer finance company offering unsecured personal loans, car title loans, and auto financing since 1922, with 26 locations across Oregon and Washington.
description_long:
United Finance Co. is a family-owned consumer finance company founded in 1922 with deep roots in local communities. The Beaverton location has served customers since 1975 and is one of 26 offices operating across Oregon and Washington. The company operates as a traditional installment lender, positioning itself as an alternative to payday loans with longer terms and more affordable monthly payments.

United Finance offers unsecured personal loans, car title loans (secured loans using a vehicle as collateral), new and used auto financing, and debt consolidation loans. They explicitly state they work with customers who have bad credit, previous bankruptcies, charge-offs, or collections accounts. The company reports to all three major credit bureaus and emphasizes helping customers rebuild credit. Loan amounts appear to range within typical personal loan parameters, though specific maximums are not listed on the website.

The company distinguishes itself through in-person service, direct relationships with local auto dealers and furniture stores, and a stated commitment to saying "yes" when other lenders may decline. Staff are bilingual (Se Habla Español noted at multiple locations), and customer reviews highlight individual staff members by name, suggesting personalized service. The Beaverton office is staffed by Rick, who receives multiple mentions in customer testimonials. The company operates traditional business hours (9AM-6PM weekdays) with phone and fax support.

While United Finance serves a legitimate niche for credit-challenged borrowers, the website provides minimal pricing transparency—no APR ranges, fees, or loan terms are disclosed. This is common in the installment loan industry but represents a significant information gap. Customer reviews are consistently positive and recent, suggesting operational stability, though the lack of publicly available rate information means borrowers must contact offices directly for actual loan costs.
address: 10870 SW Beaverton Hillsdale Hwy, Beaverton, OR 97005

company_info.headquarters: 10870 SW Beaverton Hillsdale Hwy, Beaverton, OR 97005

pros: Established family-owned company operating since 1922 with 26 locations, providing stability and local presence
Willing to work with borrowers with bad credit, previous bankruptcies, charge-offs, and collections accounts
Longer terms and more affordable monthly payments compared to payday loans
Reports to all three major credit bureaus, helping customers rebuild credit history
Bilingual staff (Spanish language support) at multiple locations
Works directly with local auto dealers and furniture stores for financing purchases
Personalized service with identifiable staff members and positive recent customer reviews (2023-2024)
cons: No APR ranges, fees, or specific loan terms disclosed on website—borrowers must call or visit to learn costs
Website lacks information about maximum loan amounts or eligibility criteria
Limited online application process mentioned but not detailed; primarily requires in-person visits or phone calls
As a secured/unsecured lender, interest rates likely higher than traditional banks due to borrower risk profile
No information about online account management, payment portals, or digital convenience features
best_for: Borrowers with bad credit, previous bankruptcy, or delinquencies who cannot qualify for traditional bank loans
People seeking auto financing who prefer working with local lenders rather than dealership finance departments
Customers needing debt consolidation with flexible terms and personalized service
Bilingual borrowers (Spanish speakers) seeking accessible local financial services
diagnosis: United Finance is best for credit-challenged borrowers in Oregon/Washington who need flexible installment loans and value in-person, personalized service over online convenience. The primary caveat is complete lack of pricing transparency on their website—you must contact them directly to learn actual APR, fees, and terms, and rates will likely be significantly higher than traditional bank loans due to their target borrower profile.
services: Car title loans (secured with vehicle collateral)
Furniture and retail purchase financing (through partnerships with local stores)
Loans for customers with bad credit or damaged credit history
Loans for customers with previous bankruptcies or charge-offs
Credit bureau reporting to all three major bureaus
